### Post by thadiusdean on 2007-01-28
I'll press the combination, the screen will "pop" black, and the pop back to reveal a jumbled mess of colors which I assume is my screen. Here's my xorg.conf file
```
# /etc/X11/xorg.conf (xorg X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using
# values from the debconf database.
```

Has anyone else had this problem before?

### Post by crispy_420 on 2007-01-30
Are you logging out of X and trying to restart after a change?

If so I believe this is known issue with ATI drivers, atleast I know it is with proprietary drivers. But I see you are not using them. Maybe an across the board ATI driver issue.

For me, I'm using fglrx drivers, I can't log out and back in. Say for example I want to use KDE instead of Gnome, I have to reboot. If I log out, I'm dead in the water. Keyboard, command line, everything dead. That forces a hard shutdown. I have yet to find a fix and it looks as ATI is not addressing it.

---

### Post by thadiusdean on 2007-02-01
Yeah, I'm using the radeon drivers I believe. But I've had this problem with fglrx as well.
